steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content:
Cube then add.
$$k(2)=8+110=118$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. Substituting $2$ for $x$ in the given equation yields $k(2)={2}^{3}+110$, which is equivalent to $k(2)=8+110$, or $k(2)=118$. Therefore, the value of $k(x)$ when $x=2$ is $118$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $k(x)$ when $x=2$ if $k(x)=3x+110$, not $k(x)={x}^{3}+110$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $k(x)$ when $x=2$ if $k(x)=x+3+110$, not $k(x)={x}^{3}+110$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $k(x)$ when $x=0$, not $x=2$.
